prose: **Aniline Fukui Reactivity** — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`, PySCF with density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz (fixed, not relaxed). SCF converged; Fukui indices computed from neutral, cation (N+1, doublet), and anion (N−1, doublet) species using Mulliken charge differences. Charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0, Σf⁻ = 1.0). **Most electrophilic site: C atom (ortho/para position, atom index 2, 1-based label 3), f⁺ = 2.298 (electron gain index).** Most nucleophilic site: N atom (atom index 0), f⁻ = 0.205 (electron loss index). Full Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) and per-atom Mulliken charges in neutral/cation/anion states are in the result JSON and summary table above. Fukui method is a conceptual density functional tool predicting reactivity hotspots via finite-difference derivatives of electron density with respect to electron number; it does not predict reaction barriers or rates, only local susceptibility. Command: `chemkit fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 aniline.xyz`.
